title: Battle of Khar Delba
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Conjecture}} {{Battle |prev=[[Second raid on the Sith Citadel]][[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 4|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 4]] |conc= |next=[[Skirmish over Phaegon III]]''[[Crosscurrent]]'' |image=[[File:BattleOfKharDelba1.jpg]] |name=Battle of Khar Delba |conflict=None (isolated conflict; preceding the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]])''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' |date=[[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]] |place=[[Khar Delba]] |result=Victory for [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow's]] forces[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 5|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 5]] |side1=Naga Sadow's forces |side2=[[Ludo Kressh's faction|Ludo Kressh's forces]] |side3= |side4= |commanders1=Naga Sadow |commanders2=*[[Ludo Kressh]] *[[Horak-mul]] † *[[Dor Gal-ram]] † |commanders3= |commanders4= |unit1= |unit2= |unit3= |unit4= |forces1=*Turrets *[[Khar Shian]] [[Derriphan-class battleship|''Derriphan''-class battleships]] *[[Khar Shian]] [[Sith starfighter|fighters]] |forces2=*Kressh's flagship *Horak-mul's battleship *Dor Gal-ram's battleship *Several other ''Derriphan''-class battleships |forces3= |forces4= |casual1=Minimal |casual2=*Horak-mul *Dor Gal-ram |casual3= |casual4= |civilian= }} {{Quote|You struck at the ''wrong fortress'', Ludo Kressh! This is just a decoy—all my defenses have always been on the dark side of the moon. Your spies have failed you. ''Everything'' has failed you, Ludo. You never should have imagined your petty scheme could surpass my grand dreams… as you shall see…|Naga Sadow|}} A '''battle''' on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Khar Delba]] took place in the year [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], and served to consolidate the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow's]] power before he brought the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]] into the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]] against the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Following the [[Death/Legends|death]] of Dark Lord [[Marka Ragnos]], control of the Sith Empire was in contention between prominent [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]] [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]] and [[Ludo Kressh]]. The arrival in [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]]-controlled [[Realspace/Legends|space]] of two explorers from the Republic, [[Gav Daragon|Gav]] and [[Jori Daragon]], caused the two factions a great deal of alarm, leading to an inevitable conflict between Kressh and Sadow. Kressh, who believed that the Daragons' arrival signaled an impending invasion by Republic forces, advised caution, while Sadow saw an opportunity to expand the Sith Empire's boundaries. Sadow managed to play upon the paranoia of the other Sith Lords, to the effect that he was named the new Dark Lord of the Sith in order to deal with this perceived threat. Although most of the [[Sith Council]] backed Sadow, Kressh and his followers, [[Horak-mul]] and [[Dor Gal-ram]], refused to accept Sadow's legitimacy. When Kressh's [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] attacked Sadow's citadel on Khar Delba, the new Dark Lord was prepared, having amassed a secret fleet that he hid at his true headquarters on Khar Delba's [[Moon/Legends|moon]], [[Khar Shian]]. The opposition fleet was caught off-guard and defeated, while Sadow allowed Jori Daragon to escape back to the Republic, carrying a locator beacon that would allow Sadow to lead a Sith invasion there in what would become the Great Hyperspace War. ==History== ===Background=== {{Quote|Gather your forces! With your military might, your fleets of ships, we can make a surprise attack. We can stop this insanity that will bring about the downfall of our entire empire! We must stop Naga Sadow before it is too late!|Ludo Kressh|}} The [[Death/Legends|death]] of the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Marka Ragnos]] in [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]] left a power vacuum in the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]], with the two most prominent [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]], [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]] and [[Ludo Kressh]], vying for control. Following [[Funeral of Marka Ragnos|Ragnos's funeral]] on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], Sadow and Kressh faced off in a duel with [[Sith sword]]s, only to be interrupted by the [[Force ghost/Legends|specter]] of Ragnos himself, who warned the two of an uncertain future for the insular Sith Empire, regardless of its auspicious past and present golden age.[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 2]] Precluding immediate further conflict between the two [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] was the arrival of a strange [[Starship/Legends|starship]] on Korriban—the ''[[Starbreaker 12]]'', flown by down-on-their-luck explorers [[Gav Daragon|Gav]] and [[Jori Daragon]]. The Daragons, native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Empress Teta/Legends|Koros Major]] in the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], had entered random coordinates into their [[Starship/Legends|ship's]] [[Navigation computer/Legends|navicomputer]] as a last-ditch effort to make enough [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|money]] to earn their keep. This first contact between [[Human/Legends|Humans]] from the Republic and the [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]] was met with suspicion by the Sith leaders. After ordering their guards to take the two youths [[Prison/Legends|prisoner]], Sadow and Kressh disagreed over how to interpret their arrival. Kressh feared an impending [[Alien/Legends|alien]] invasion, while Sadow wanted to learn more of where the explorers had come from, in the hopes that the Sith Empire could expand outside of the space they had previously explored. Sadow and Kressh, along with the rest of the Sith and their new prisoners, returned to the Sith throneworld of [[Ziost/Legends|Ziost]].[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 3]] ====Sadow's deception==== [[File:BattleOfKharDelba2.jpg|thumb|left|300px|[[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow's]] secret fleet lies in wait behind [[Khar Shian]].]] This disagreement was the root of a major rift between Sadow and Kressh, who continued to make bids for supreme control of the Sith Empire. Sadow was not beyond political manipulation to achieve his means, however, and managed through staging a false escape by Jori Daragon to convince Kressh and the [[Sith Council]] that the Republic had attacked, warranting a retaliatory invasion of the Republic. Gathering support of most of the Council, Sadow was elected to take up the mantle of Dark Lord of the Sith in order to meet this new "threat." Kressh, however, refused to acknowledge Sadow's legitimacy, and left with two of his supporters, [[Horak-mul]] and [[Dor Gal-ram]]. Regardless of Kressh's challenge, the majority of the Sith accepted Sadow as their new master and allowed him to undertake the marking ritual, branding his forehead with the mark of the Dark Lord. Sadow continued to plot against Kressh, keeping his own goals foremost in his mind. After the staged attack and "escape," the new Dark Lord moved Jori to his citadel on [[Khar Delba]]. Gav, whom Sadow had taken under his wing and begun to train in the use of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]], was in an identical stronghold on Khar Delba's [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Khar Shian]], where Sadow also had clandestinely amassed a [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] of [[Warship/Legends|warships]] under his direct command. Realizing that he would need Jori Daragon at least to lead him to the Republic, Sadow sent warriors of the [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] caste to steal the ''Starbreaker 12'' from [[Ziost/Legends|Ziost]], instructing the warriors to leave behind evidence with his personal symbol on it—proof that Sadow was behind the attacks. Kressh took the bait, and gathered his followers for an assault on Khar Delba. ===The battle=== ====Kressh's forces attack==== [[File:BattleOfKharDelba3.jpg|thumb|right|275px|[[Ludo Kressh]]'s forces attack the citadel on [[Khar Delba]].]] {{Quote|Quick! Destroy Naga Sadow! The stain of his treachery must be obliterated from the Sith Empire!''"
"''His defenses can't withstand our attack. We've caught him off guard!''"
"''We're with you, Ludo Kressh.|Ludo Kressh, Horak-mul, and Dor Gal-ram|}} Kressh brought a fleet of [[Derriphan-class battleship|''Derriphan''-class battleships]] to Khar Delba, supported by Horak-mul and Dor Gal-ram, and immediately commenced an attack upon Sadow's fortress. Orders were issued that no transmissions would be sent to the surface, and that surrender was not an option available to Sadow. The Dark Lord of the Sith, however, was pleased with this outcome, as he had anticipated it and made appropriate plans. As his rivals' ships [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|bombarded]] the fortress, Sadow deceived Jori Daragon, instructing his warriors to man the turrets and put up a defense. Kressh and his allies bought into this, as well, fully believing that the fortress's defenses were all Sadow had in store for them. Sadow, knowing that he had found his opportunity, urged Jori to take the ''Starbreaker 12'' and escape back to the Republic. Although she expressed dismay over leaving her brother behind in the Sith Empire, Jori allowed herself to be convinced when Sadow lied that Kressh had come to interrogate her in order to gather information about the Republic. The ''Starbreaker 12'' blasted out of the Khar Delba fortress amid a hail of cannon fire between the turrets and Kressh's attacking battleships. Kressh, recognizing the vessel, instructed his forces to shoot it down. Although Horak-mul managed to bring the ''Starbreaker 12'' into his sights, Jori managed to maneuver away, jumping to the safety of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. ====Sadow's double-cross==== {{Quote|What?''"
"''But you are my ''crew''! My ''slaves''!|Horak-mul and Dor Gal-ram, betrayed by their crewmen|}} [[File:BattleOfKharDelba4.jpg|thumb|left|150px|[[Horak-mul]] is betrayed.]] As soon as Jori Daragon was safely away, Sadow summoned his massive fleet from the shadow of Khar Shian, ordering them to begin their attack. Caught completely off-guard, Kressh was livid. Sadow transmitted to his rival's ship, taunting him for his gullibility as he reached to send yet another transmission, this time to the ships of Horak-mul and Dor Gal-ram. The Massassi warriors on both vessels, secretly part of the [[Sadow'een]], an order of assassins loyal to Sadow,{{TORcite|type=class|Sith Inquisitor|Bitter Rivalries|Hoth}} turned on and murdered their masters, taking control of Horak-mul's and Dor Gal-ram's battleships and in turn firing upon Kressh's remaining fleet. All the while, Sadow's fighters and battleships of his secret fleet hammered away at those of his rival. Kressh saw no chance for victory, and ordered the remnants of his forces to retreat. ===Outcome=== {{Quote|Yes, fly to your unsuspecting Republic… You were meant to escape alone, Jori Daragon. Now I can set the rest of my plan in motion!|Naga Sadow|}} With the defeat of Ludo Kressh, nothing stood in the way of Naga Sadow's complete control of the Sith Empire. Returning to his real fortress on Khar Shian, Sadow was greeting by throngs of his loyal servants and warriors, reveling in his victory through guile. Gav learned of his sister's escape, but did not know that Sadow had orchestrated it all for his own benefit—the ''Starbreaker 12'' had been fitted with a tracking device, giving the Sith a route into Republic [[Realspace/Legends|space]] for Sadow's planned invasion. Although defeated, Kressh continued to resist Sadow, but his efforts were in vain—when Kressh's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] arrived above Khar Delba once more, Sadow managed to trick Gav Daragon into destroying the vessel.[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 1|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 1]] Although this proved to be deception on Kressh's part—the craft had actually been a decoy, much as Sadow had previously tricked Kressh into attacking a decoy fortress—Kressh would never again gain the upper hand against Sadow, and was eventually killed by Sadow's forces in [[Space warfare/Legends|space combat]].[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 5|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 5]] After gathering his forces, Sadow was able to follow Jori Daragon back to the Republic, launching an invasion that would become known as the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]]. ==Behind the scenes== The Battle of Khar Delba, a conflict in which Naga Sadow manages to secure control of the Sith Empire, appeared in the [[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 4|fourth]] and [[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 5|fifth]] issues of ''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith]]'', a [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] story arc written by ''[[Star Wars]]'' author [[Kevin J. Anderson]] and published in [[1996]]{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54589/star_wars_tales_of_the_jedi_-_the_golden_age_of_the_sith_1996|text=Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith (1996) #0}} and [[1997]]{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54594/star_wars_tales_of_the_jedi_-_the_golden_age_of_the_sith_1996_5|text=Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith (1996) #5}} as part of the larger [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi'']] comic series. Both issues were drawn by artist [[Dario Carrasco, Jr.]]. Anderson had wanted to write a history of the Sith, and was able to obtain some information from [[George Lucas]] to create a base for his work.[[Wookieepedia:Interview/Kevin J. Anderson|Interview with Kevin J. Anderson]] The events of the Great Hyperspace War were laid out in the next story arc of the series, [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire|''Tales of the Jedi: The Fall of the Sith Empire'']], also written by Anderson.''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire]]'' ==Appearances== *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 4|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 4]] {{1st}} *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 5|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 5]] *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' {{Codex}} ==Sources== *[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] *''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]'' *''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *{{InsiderCite|88|Heritage of the Sith}} *[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] *{{TORweb|url=holonet/galactic-history|text=Timeline 12: The Great Hyperspace War|int=Timeline 12: The Great Hyperspace War}} {{Mo}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Schlacht von Khar Delba#legends |es=Batalla de Khar Delba }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Khar Delba, Battle of}} [[Category:Battles during the Manderon Period]] [[Category:Battles during the post-Manderon period]] [[Category:Battles in the Sith Worlds]] [[Category:Orbital bombardments]]
